name: Robledo y Peñascosa — circular desde Balazote
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Balazote traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and rural roads, offering varied elevation changes. The region features areas like Peñas de San Pedro and Peñascosa, suggesting a terrain with notable climbs and descents. Road cyclists can expect a mix of open countryside and routes that wind through more elevated areas. Best road cycling routes around Balazote The most popular road cycling route is Robledo y Peñascosa — circular desde Balazote, a 58.9 miles (94.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 5 minutes to complete. This circular route offers a comprehensive exploration of the local countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Peñas de San Pedro – Peñas de San Pedro loop from Argamasón, a moderate 37.9 miles (60.9 km) path. This route loops around the Peñas de San Pedro area, providing views of the surrounding hills. Local road cyclists also love the Church of Peñascosa – From Pesebre to Cilleruelo loop from San Pedro, a 56.0 miles (90.1 km) trail leading through the Peñascosa region, often completed in about 4 hours 17 minutes. Road cycling around Balazote is defined by its hilly terrain, rural roads, and varied elevation. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Balazote are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Balazote's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general terrain like for road cycling around Balazote?
A: Road cycling routes around Balazote traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and rural roads. You can expect varied elevation changes, with routes winding through open countryside and more elevated areas like Peñas de San Pedro and Peñascosa, which feature notable climbs and descents.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners in Balazote?
A: The road cycling routes around Balazote are generally suited for cyclists with some experience, as they range from moderate to difficult in terms of challenge. There are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in this guide, due to the hilly terrain and varied elevation.

Q: How long do the road cycling routes around Balazote typically take to complete?
A: The duration of road cycling routes around Balazote varies depending on the specific trail and your pace. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1168934983/robledo-y-penascosa-circular-desde-balazote" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledo y Peñascosa — circular desde Balazote&lt;/a&gt; is approximately 58.9 miles (94.7 km) and typically takes around 4 hours 5 minutes. Longer, more challenging routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20130261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas de San Pedro – Bogarra loop from Balazote&lt;/a&gt;, at 83.8 miles (134.9 km), can take over 6 hours.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available from Balazote?
A: Yes, there are several circular road cycling routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1168934983/robledo-y-penascosa-circular-desde-balazote" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledo y Peñascosa — circular desde Balazote&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a comprehensive exploration of the local countryside. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1285976" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas de San Pedro – Peñas de San Pedro loop from Argamasón&lt;/a&gt;, providing views of the surrounding hills.

Q: What kind of challenges can advanced road cyclists expect in Balazote?
A: Advanced road cyclists will find several challenging routes around Balazote, characterized by significant elevation gains and longer distances. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129772" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Peñascosa – From Pesebre to Cilleruelo loop from San Pedro&lt;/a&gt; (56.0 miles / 90.1 km with over 1200m elevation gain) and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20130261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas de San Pedro – Bogarra loop from Balazote&lt;/a&gt; (83.8 miles / 134.9 km with over 1700m elevation gain) are classified as difficult and offer demanding rides.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Balazote?
A: While specific data isn't available, the region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, offering milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, while winters are typically cooler. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or notable landmarks along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass through open countryside and elevated areas, providing scenic views of the surrounding hills. You might also encounter interesting structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Masegoso River Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features parts of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1003575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of the Sierra de Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a different perspective on the landscape.

Q: Where can I find parking for road biking in Balazote?
A: While specific parking areas aren't detailed for each route, most road cycling routes around Balazote typically start from or near towns and villages. It's advisable to look for public parking facilities in Balazote or the starting points of specific routes, such as Argamasón or San Pedro, before your ride.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Balazote?
A: The komoot community highly rates road cycling in Balazote, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the challenge of the rolling hills, and the opportunity to explore rural roads and areas like Peñas de San Pedro and Peñascosa.

Q: Are there any facilities like cafes or rest areas along the road cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes are not listed for every route, the routes often pass through or near small towns and villages where you can find places to refuel. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961348" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Santanas Rest Area&lt;/a&gt; is a designated facility that might be accessible on some routes, offering a spot to take a break.

Q: How many road cycling routes are covered in this guide for Balazote?
A: This guide covers 6 distinct road cycling routes around Balazote, offering a range of options for moderate to difficult ability levels.
tips:
Without a doubt, the Sanctuary of Cortes is the most visited place in the province of Albacete.
In 1976, an article by W. A. Christian on Spanish sanctuaries had been published; in it he made a classification of the importance of these centers of popular devotion, today also called local, based on the geographical area occupied by the devotees who went to each one of them; established four national sanctuaries and a dozen regional ones; Among the latter was that of the Virgin of Cortes de Alcaraz, this being the only one in the Castilian-La Mancha region that was considered to be of this category. From this, without a doubt, it was inferred that the Cortes was a very significant sanctuary, and today it maintains that character.
Road with great views of the Sierra de Alcaraz. Asphalt seems freshly laid (mid-2022), it's a real pleasure to cycle here.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of Our Lady of Cortes
